message:
chore(cleanup): drop debug println!s, tighten unwrap()s, modernize if-let chains

A scrub-pass across the cli + server crates with no behavior change.

cli/src/legacy.rs
  - Restructure `find_installed_am_binary` so the #[cfg(test)] short
    return body sits in its own braced arm. The previous shape (an
    early `return None;` followed by the real implementation in the
    same function body) was technically fine but tripped reviewers
    every time, because the "real" code looked dead under #[cfg(test)].
  - Drop two `println!` debug breadcrumbs from `detect_env_marker` —
    leftover instrumentation from chasing an env-marker bug, not
    intended for production output.

cli/src/lib.rs
  - Collapse three nested `if include_X { if let Some(obj) = ... { ... } }`
    blocks into the equivalent `if include_X && let Some(obj) = ...`
    form (now stable). Pure formatting / nesting reduction.
  - Drop two `println!("healthy: {}", healthy);` lines that were
    accidentally left in front of `assert!` statements in the
    sidecar-compat health-check tests; the assert message already
    surfaces the value on failure.

cli/src/e2e_runner.rs
  - Replace `.unwrap()` on `strip_prefix("test_")` and
    `strip_suffix(".sh")` with `.expect("already checked prefix"/
    "already checked suffix")` (we just matched the same pattern with
    `name.starts_with("test_") && name.ends_with(".sh")`, so the
    .expect documents the local invariant).
  - Same treatment for the ANSI-strip regex compile in `Runner`:
    `regex::Regex::new(...).unwrap()` →
    `regex::Regex::new(...).expect("valid regex")`.

server/src/atc.rs
  - Hoist `use std::fmt::Write as _;` out of the inner `for j in row`
    loop in `AtcLivenessPolicyArtifact::canonical_hash_input`. Bringing
    the trait into scope per-iteration was confusing and clippy nags
    about it; one import at the top of the function is the same code.

server/src/tui_chrome.rs
  - In the `tab_window_plan_keeps_all_screens_visible` test, bump
    `nominal_tab_width` from 16 → 20. The current widest tab plus
    its separator already exceeds 16, and 20 = label cap (12) +
    2-digit key + padding + 1-cell separator, which matches the
    documented short-label cap. With the old value the test was
    starting to tip into "marginal" territory whenever a new screen
    was added.

server/src/lib.rs
  - Drop a stray `println!("ERR IN DISPATCH: {e:?}");` from the
    `Err(e)` arm of `dispatch_compose_envelope`. The very next line
    already does `tracing::error!("compose: failed to dispatch
    message: {e}");`, which is the right channel.

Untracked artifact `test_env_read` (a 4.1 MB ELF) is intentionally
not staged — it's a one-off scratch binary.
